acceptanceSignals: Vendors can manage menu items, prices, images, and daily quotas.
Quota accounting prevents oversell under concurrent ordering.
Configured preorder window and cutoff rules are enforced in ordering APIs.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Menu Supply and Window Management in 0 attempts

lastCommit.message: feat: implement Menu Supply and Window Management (attempt 3)

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The active policy is satisfied for this feature. The workspace shows synchronized HTTP-contract changes in the same change set (`src/contract.rs`, `contract/openapi/openapi.json`, `contract/openapi/openapi.yaml`, and `contract/generated/ts-client/*`), route-to-spec parity coverage via `runtime_http_route_catalog_matches_openapi_contract` in `tests/openapi_contract_platform.rs`, CI enforcement in `.github/workflows/openapi-contract.yml` (`cargo test --test openapi_contract_platform` + `pnpm run contract:verify`), and explicit tests validating error-code to error-schema mappings for the ordering/menu endpoints.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: Current workspace satisfies the active policy for this feature: vendor menu management includes price/image/daily quota, quota accounting is atomic under concurrency, ordering APIs enforce Taipei-based preorder and cutoff governance with bounded vendor overrides, and special requests remain controlled enum-only with bounded size. No legacy/fallback branch preserving superseded behavior was found in this feature surface.

excludedApproaches: Using non-atomic quota updates that allow oversell.
Defaulting to unrestricted free-text special requests.
executionConstraints: Default preorder open window supports up to 7 days ahead with policy-bounded vendor overrides.
Default modify/cancel cutoff is previous day 17:00 Asia/Taipei with bounded vendor override.
Special-request schema must remain controlled and risk-limited pending final clarification.
name: Menu Supply and Window Management
rationale: Supply and time-window correctness is central to order predictability.
summary: Enable safe menu management, quota control, and cutoff governance.
